---
title: "Data Sources"
subtitle: "Where the data lives and how to access it"
---

The labs use 2 datasets. Both are stored on **S3-compatible object storage** hosted on DigitalOcean Spaces, not AWS. This is intentional: it demonstrates that S3 is a protocol, not just an AWS product.

::: {.callout-note title="S3-compatible storage"}
Amazon S3 defined the API for object storage (PUT, GET, list, presigned URLs). Many providers implement the same API: DigitalOcean Spaces, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2. Any tool that speaks S3 (DuckDB's `httpfs`, `boto3`, `s3cmd`, `aws s3`) works with all of them by changing the endpoint URL.

For these labs, the data lives at `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/`. You can read it via plain HTTPS (no credentials needed) or via the S3 protocol with a custom endpoint.
:::

## Datasets

The labs reference these datasets by URL in their SQL and Python scripts. You do not need to download anything manually.

### 1. Rainfall data (from DSCI 525 milestones)

The same Sydney rainfall dataset you used throughout the course. ~23 MB CSV, 26 climate models, daily observations.

| Format | URL | Size |
|---|---|---|
| CSV | `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/ml_data_SYD.csv` | ~23 MB |

**Used in**: Lab 1 (Bruin pipeline), Lab 2 (cloud queries)

### 2. Open Food Facts (curated subset)

Product data from [Open Food Facts](https://world.openfoodfacts.org/), filtered to products with complete nutrition information. 22 columns covering product identity, nutrition scores, and per-100g nutrient values.

| Format | URL | Size | Rows |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full (parquet) | `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_sample.parquet` | ~189 MB | ~4.4M |
| All 50K (parquet) | `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_sample_50k.parquet` | ~4 MB | 50,000 (10K per grade) |
| Batch 1 (parquet) | `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_batch_1.parquet` | ~1.3 MB | 20,000 (4K per grade) |
| Batch 2 (parquet) | `https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_batch_2.parquet` | ~1.9 MB | 30,000 (6K per grade) |

Batch 1 contains clean products with no edge cases. Batch 2 includes products where the grade does not match the nutrition numbers (grade "a" with high sugar, grade "e" with low energy). The 50K file is batch 1 + batch 2 combined. Lab 1 uses these batches to demonstrate pipeline re-runs with changing data.

**Columns**: `code`, `product_name_en`, `brands`, `categories`, `nutriscore_grade` (A-E), `nutriscore_score`, `nova_group` (1-4), `ecoscore_grade`, `ecoscore_score`, `ingredients_n`, `additives_n`, `lang`, `countries_tags`, `scans_n`, `energy_kcal_100g`, `fat_100g`, `saturated_fat_100g`, `carbohydrates_100g`, `sugars_100g`, `proteins_100g`, `salt_100g`, `fiber_100g`

**Used in**: Lab 1 (batch 1 + batch 2), Lab 2 (cloud analytics), Lab 3 (embeddings), Connecting project (50K or full)

### Reading data in DuckDB

```sql
-- Plain HTTPS (no credentials, works everywhere)
SELECT * FROM read_parquet(
    'https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_sample_50k.parquet'
) LIMIT 5;
```

```sql
-- S3 protocol (same data, demonstrates S3 compatibility)
SET s3_endpoint = 'tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com';
SET s3_region = 'tor1';
SET s3_access_key_id = '';     -- public bucket, empty credentials
SET s3_secret_access_key = '';

SELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://mds26/data/525next-steps/food_sample_50k.parquet') LIMIT 5;
```

### Reading data in Python

```python
import pandas as pd

url = "https://mds26.tor1.digitaloceanspaces.com/data/525next-steps/food_sample_50k.parquet"
df = pd.read_parquet(url)
```
